findings.issue: The amendment reverses the Presidency Part V no-duty holding.
findings.reviewerReply: The conflict is real and cannot be construed away. Section 13.1 permits the chambers to amend cadence prospectively while preserving the first window and all evidentiary gates.

findings.issue: RR-8 prices first-window non-commencement as lawful history.
findings.reviewerReply: The remedial duty does not retroactively relabel 2279-2288 and therefore does not contradict RR-8.

findings.issue: Compelled commencement could become compelled certification.
findings.reviewerReply: Certification, failure, and recognized void remain available; no rate or evidentiary threshold changes.
Replacement constitution, public attribution, and Meritboard sanction close the dissolution route.

presidency.reason: The chambers used section 13.1 to reverse the no-duty rule prospectively, preserved the first window, and left factual certification entirely with the locked instrument.
